findings.title: Length-delimited parser bypasses frame boundary when 32-bit size overflows signed int
findings.description: The length prefix is read as uint32_t, then cast to int in PushLimit(static_cast<int>(size)) and again in the final position check. If an attacker supplies a delimited size greater than INT_MAX, PushLimit() silently refuses to install the boundary because the argument becomes negative, but MergeFromCodedStream() still runs against the caller's outer stream limit. The function eventually returns false, yet it may already have consumed bytes past the declared frame, which can desynchronize concatenated-message parsers and bypass the intended per-message boundary.

findings.title: Packed fixed-size repeated field growth lacks checked addition before resize
findings.description: After deriving new_entries from attacker-controlled packed-field length, the code resizes/reserves with old_entries + new_entries using signed int arithmetic and no explicit overflow guard. RepeatedField::resize() only DCHECKs that the requested size is non-negative in ResizeImpl(), so release builds rely on the sum not wrapping. A message that repeatedly appends packed data to an already large repeated field can drive signed overflow here, risking undersized allocation bookkeeping before ReadRaw() or AddAlreadyReserved() writes element data.

findings.title: upb packed-field decoder grows arrays with unchecked size_t addition
findings.description: The upb decoder computes the requested capacity as arr->size + elem without checking for size_t wraparound before calling _upb_Array_Realloc(). This helper is used on input-derived counts in packed decode paths such as _upb_Decoder_DecodeFixedPacked() (count from val->size >> lg2) and the per-element growth loops in _upb_Decoder_DecodeVarintPacked()/_upb_Decoder_DecodeEnumPacked(). On large or incremental attacker-controlled repeated fields, especially on 32-bit builds, the unchecked addition can wrap to a smaller capacity while subsequent code still increments arr->size and memcpy()s parsed elements, creating a potential out-of-bounds write condition.

patterns_checked: unchecked size casts into int-based parsing APIs
